title: Boat Rental in Reunion: A Day on the Water in 2026
html:
For a memorable day on the water in Reunion, set out from Saint-Gilles les Bains, where you can rent a motorboat or catamaran for around 150 EUR to 800 EUR. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Reunion or list your own.

Where to set off in Reunion

The main departure hub is Port de Plaisance de Saint-Gilles les Bains, which offers pontoon berths and fuel facilities. Other launch points include Passe de l'Étang Salé, a channel for small craft near Saint-Leu, and the fishing harbour of Saint-Pierre. For calmer conditions, the sheltered bay of Boucan Canot provides easy access to the west coast reefs. Further east, the marina at Sainte-Marie serves as a base for trips around Île aux Biches.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (6, 8m), ideal for exploring the coast between Saint-Gilles and Saint-Leu, accommodates up to 6 passengers, from 150 EUR half-day.
  2. Catamaran (12, 15m), perfect for full-day family outings with snorkelling stops, sleeps 8, 12, from 500 EUR full-day.
  3. Sports boat (Axopar 28), a fast rigid inflatable for towable toys and dolphin watching, up to 8 guests, from 350 EUR half-day.
  4. Motor yacht (Fairline Targa 43), a spacious cabin cruiser with galley and sunbathing area, up to 10 guests, from 800 EUR full-day, see the Fairline Targa 43 charter page for details.
  5. Rib tender (4, 5m), a nimble option for short trips to remote coves, max 4 guests, from 100 EUR per hour.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Reunion?

Prices start at 100 EUR for a shared tour and rise to 900 EUR for a crewed luxury yacht.

  • Shared group tour (2, 3 hours), 50 EUR per person, often around the coral gardens of La Saline.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, unskippered), 150, 250 EUR, fuel extra.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, skippered with lunch), 500, 700 EUR for the vessel.
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day (Fairline Targa 43 or similar), 700, 900 EUR, including crew and fuel.

For the best selection of boats at these rates, compare verified boat listings for your dates.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Reunion?

For powered rental boats over 6hp, a valid coastal licence or equivalent is required unless the boat is skippered. The French ‘permis côtier’ (coastal permit) is formally needed, but many charter operators include a skipper for safety. Always confirm exact requirements with the operator when booking; BoatHire24 listings note whether a licence is needed.

Tip: book a morning departure to avoid the trade wind, the ‘alizés’ pick up after 2pm, making the return choppier along the west coast.

What is the best season for boating in Reunion?

The best season runs from April to November, when the southeast trade winds are moderate and the sea is calmest. This window avoids the cyclone season (December to March) and gives reliable conditions for a full-day trip. In September and October, visibility underwater is excellent for snorkelling around the coral reserves.

At a glance

  • Price band: 100 EUR to 900 EUR per day
  • Peak months: April to November
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Reunion

Can you anchor anywhere along the coast?

You may anchor in sandy bottoms west of Saint-Gilles, but avoid the marine protected areas around Cap La Houssaye, marked by yellow buoys.

Is it possible to rent a boat without a skipper?

Yes, you can hire small motorboats as bareboat if you hold a valid boating licence accepted in French waters.

How long does it take to motor from Saint-Gilles to Saint-Leu?

The distance is about 7 nautical miles, taking 30, 40 minutes at 12 knots, depending on sea state.

Can you see dolphins on a day charter in Reunion?

Spinner dolphins are frequently sighted off the west coast, especially in the morning near the Passe de l'Étang Salé channel.

When you have a boat in Reunion, consider offering it to travellers by becoming a host. This simple process gives your vessel visibility to thousands of renters worldwide. Become a host on BoatHire24 and start earning from your boat today.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Reunion and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
